PaymentVoucher.title: PaymentVoucher
PaymentVoucher.description: Represents a single payment voucher in the Tropical Ledger system, capturing all details from the digital data entry form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentVoucher entity.

voucherNumber.description: The unique identifying number assigned to the payment voucher.

voucherDate.description: The specific date when the payment voucher was issued.

paidTo.description: The name of the person or entity to whom the payment is being made.

amountRO.description: The numerical value of the payment amount in Rial Omani (R.O.).

amountROWords.description: The payment amount in Rial Omani (R.O.) expressed in words, for formal documentation.

amountBz.description: The numerical value of the payment amount in Baisa (Bz), the fractional currency of Rial Omani.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment, such as 'Cash', 'Cheque', or 'Bank Transfer'.

bankName.description: The name of the bank involved in the transaction, applicable for cheque or bank transfer payments.

referenceNumber.description: A specific reference number for the transaction, such as a cheque number or bank transfer ID.

purpose.description: A detailed description of the reason or purpose for the payment, often labeled as 'Being' on physical vouchers.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created or last modified this payment voucher. (Relationship: User 1:N PaymentVoucher)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this payment voucher record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this payment voucher record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores all PaymentVoucher documents for the Tropical Ledger system. Each document includes fields like 'userId' for auditing (who created it), 'createdAt', and 'updatedAt'. Access (read/write) to this collection is granted to users whose UIDs are present in the '/authorizedUsers' collection, ensuring Authorization Independence and supporting Queryable Access Patterns for the dashboard view.

params.name: paymentVoucherId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific payment voucher document.

definition.description: A collection of marker documents where each document's ID is the UID of a user authorized to access the Tropical Ledger system. The existence of a user's UID in this collection grants them read and write access to the `/paymentVouchers` collection, implementing a robust Database Access Control (DBAC) model without custom claims. This structure ensures Authorization Independence by providing a single, external source for authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of an authorized user.

firestore.reasoning: The recommended Firestore structure for the 'Tropical Ledger' payment voucher system prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in alignment with the core design principles. The primary collection, `/paymentVouchers/{paymentVoucherId}`, stores all PaymentVoucher documents. This top-level collection supports efficient Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) by allowing all authorized users to retrieve 'the full history' of vouchers directly via a single query, which is crucial for the dashboard view. Authorization Independence is achieved by completely decoupling access control for the `paymentVouchers` collection from any data stored within the vouchers themselves. Instead, authorization relies on a separate dedicated collection, `/authorizedUsers/{userId}`. Each document in this collection acts as a marker, where its ID is the UID of a 'trusted person' (an authorized employee). Firestore security rules will check for the existence of `request.auth.uid` within `/authorizedUsers` to grant read and write access to `/paymentVouchers`. This strategy avoids slow and complex `get()` calls in rules, making them robust, atomic, and easy to debug. The `userId` field within each `PaymentVoucher` document will serve solely as an audit trail (denoting the creator/last modifier) rather than for granular access control, further enhancing Authorization Independence.
